    66	}
    67	
    68	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	// 3. HELPERS
    70	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    71	
    72	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    73	  const args = {};
    74	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    75	    const token = argv[i];
    76	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    77	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    78	    }
    79	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    80	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    81	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    82	      args[key] = true;
    83	    } else {
    84	      args[key] = next;
    85	      i += 1;
    86	    }
    87	  }
    88	  return args;
    89	}
    90	
    91	function inputError(message) {
    92	  const err = new Error(message);
    93	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    94	  return err;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function ensureString(args, key) {
    98	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    99	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
   100	  }
   101	  return args[key];
   102	}
   103	
   104	function jsonOut(payload) {
   105	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
   106	}
   107	
   108	// Per-kind first state-dir env var (used for lockfile isolation across same-kind replicas).
   109	const SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND = {
   110	  'cli-codex': 'SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR',
   111	  'cli-gemini': 'SPECKIT_GEMINI_STATE_DIR',
   112	  'cli-claude-code': 'SPECKIT_CLAUDE_CODE_STATE_DIR',
   113	  'cli-opencode': 'SPECKIT_OPENCODE_STATE_DIR',
   114	  'cli-devin': 'SPECKIT_DEVIN_STATE_DIR',
   115	};
   116	
   117	/**
   118	 * Build the "run the full loop" prompt text for a CLI lineage subprocess.
   119	 * The subprocess reads this, loads the skill, and runs the loop to convergence
   120	 * with config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir overriding step_resolve_artifact_root.
   121	 */
   122	function buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage) {
   123	  const skillFile =
   124	    loopType === 'review'
   125	      ? '.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md'
   126	      : '.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md';
   127	  const agentName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review' : 'deep-research';
   128	  return [
   129	    `You are a ${agentName} agent running a fan-out lineage.`,
   130	    ``,
   131	    `Read ${skillFile} and execute the ${loopType} loop with these parameters:`,
   132	    `  spec_folder: ${specFolder}`,
   133	    `  config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: ${lineageDir}`,
   134	    `  session_id: ${sessionId}`,
   135	    `  executor: ${lineage.kind} model=${lineage.model || 'default'}`,
   136	    `  loop_type: ${loopType}`,
   137	    ``,
   138	    `The step_resolve_artifact_root step will bind artifact_dir to ${lineageDir} via the`,
   139	    `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir override — do NOT run the resolveArtifactRoot node`,
   140	    `command; bind artifact_dir directly to the override value.`,
   141	    ``,
   142	    `Run phase_init, phase_main_loop (to convergence), and phase_synthesis.`,
   143	    `Write all outputs to ${lineageDir}. Do not touch any path outside ${lineageDir}.`,
   144	    ``,
   145	    `When complete, output a single line: FANOUT_LINEAGE_COMPLETE:${lineage.label}`,
   146	  ].join('\n');
   147	}
   148	
   149	/**
   150	 * Compute a generous timeout for a full lineage loop run.
   151	 * A single iteration is timeoutSeconds; a full loop is up to iterations * timeoutSeconds.
   152	 * We double it for safety and cap at 4 hours.
   153	 */
   154	function computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage) {
   155	  const iters = lineage.iterations || 12;
   156	  const perIterSecs = lineage.timeoutSeconds || 900;
   157	  return Math.min(iters * perIterSecs * 2 * 1000, 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Build the subprocess command for a CLI lineage.
   162	 * Returns { command, args, input?, env } where input is stdin content when applicable.
   163	 */
   164	function buildLineageCommand(lineage, prompt, resolvedSandbox, resolvedPermission) {
   165	  const kind = lineage.kind;
   166	
   167	  if (kind === 'cli-codex') {
   168	    return {
   169	      command: 'codex',
   170	      args: [
   171	        'exec',
   172	        '--model',
   173	        lineage.model || 'o4-mini',
   174	        '-c',
   175	        `model_reasoning_effort=${lineage.reasoningEffort || 'medium'}`,
   176	        '-c',
   177	        `service_tier=${lineage.serviceTier || 'default'}`,
   178	        '-c',
   179	        'approval_policy=never',
   180	        '--sandbox',
   181	        resolvedSandbox,
   182	        '-',
   183	      ],
   184	      input: prompt,
   185	    };
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  if (kind === 'cli-gemini') {
   189	    return {
   190	      command: 'gemini',
   191	      args: [prompt, '-m', lineage.model || 'gemini-2.5-pro', '-s', resolvedSandbox, '-y', '-o', 'text'],
   192	    };
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  if (kind === 'cli-claude-code') {
   196	    const args = [
   197	      '-p',
   198	      prompt,
   199	      '--model',
   200	      lineage.model || 'claude-opus-4-8',
   201	      '--permission-mode',
   202	      resolvedPermission,
   203	      '--output-format',
   204	      'text',
   205	    ];
   206	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   207	      args.push('--effort', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   208	    }
   209	    return { command: 'claude', args };
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  if (kind === 'cli-opencode') {
   213	    const args = [
   214	      'run',
   215	      '--model',
   216	      lineage.model || 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-8',
   217	      '--agent',
   218	      'general',
   219	      '--format',
   220	      'json',
   221	      '--dangerously-skip-permissions',
   222	      '--pure',
   223	    ];
   224	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   225	      args.push('--variant', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   226	    }
   227	    args.push(prompt);
   228	    return { command: 'opencode', args, input: '' };
   229	  }
   230	
   231	  if (kind === 'cli-devin') {
   232	    return {
   233	      command: 'devin',
   234	      args: [
   235	        '--print',
   236	        '--model',
   237	        lineage.model || 'swe-1.6',
   238	        '--permission-mode',
   239	        resolvedPermission,
   240	      ],
   241	      input: prompt,
   242	    };
   243	  }
   244	
   245	  throw inputError(`Unknown CLI executor kind: ${kind}`);
   246	}
   247	
   248	const {
   249	  runSalvageSweep,
   250	  extractTextFromOpencodeJson,
   251	} = require('./fanout-salvage.cjs');
   252	
   253	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	// 4. CORE LOGIC
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	
   257	async function main() {
   258	  const args = parseArgs();
   259	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
   260	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   261	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   262	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   263	  }
   264	  const fanoutConfigJson = ensureString(args, 'fanoutConfigJson');
   265	  const baseArtifactDir = ensureString(args, 'baseArtifactDir');
   266	
   267	  const {
   268	    parseFanoutConfig,
   269	    expandLineages,
   270	    resolveCodexSandboxMode,
   271	    resolveClaudePermissionMode,
   272	    resolveGeminiSandboxMode,
   273	    resolveDevinPermissionMode,
   274	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts');
   275	
   276	  installSignalHandlers(() => {});
   277	  maybeThrowTestFault();
   278	
   279	  let rawConfig;
   280	  try {
   281	    rawConfig = JSON.parse(fanoutConfigJson);
   282	  } catch {
   283	    throw inputError('fanoutConfigJson is not valid JSON');
   284	  }
   285	
   286	  const fanoutConfig = parseFanoutConfig(rawConfig);
   287	  const allLineages = expandLineages(fanoutConfig);
   288	
   289	  // Fan-out pool handles CLI lineages only; native lineages are dispatched by the YAML step.
   290	  const cliLineages = allLineages.filter((l) => l.kind !== 'native');
   291	
   292	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
   293	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', results: [], summary: { total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false } });
   294	    return;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
   298	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
   299	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   300	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
   301	
   302	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
   303	
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313	
   314	      const sessionId = `fanout-${lineage.label}-${runId}`;
   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316	
   317	      const resolvedSandbox = resolveCodexSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });
   349	
   350	      // Save subprocess stdout for salvage sweep (write failures in weak CLI executors).
   351	      const logsDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'logs');
   352	      fs.mkdirSync(logsDir, { recursive: true });
   353	      const savedStdout = typeof result.stdout === 'string' ? result.stdout : '';
   354	      fs.writeFileSync(path.join(logsDir, 'fanout-lineage.out'), savedStdout, 'utf8');
   355	
   356	      // Recover missing iteration files from captured stdout when possible.
   357	      const salvage = runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout);
   358	
   359	      const exitCode = result.status ?? (result.error ? 1 : 0);
   360	      return { label: lineage.label, exitCode, timedOut: result.signal === 'SIGTERM', salvage };

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Worker Pool                                   ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Concurrency-capped fan-out primitive for the opt-in multi-executor        ║
     5	// ║ ("fan-out") layer above the single-executor deep-loop. Generalizes the    ║
     6	// ║ council parallel dispatcher (lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs) by      ║
     7	// ║ adding a concurrency cap so N executor lineages run with at most K in      ║
     8	// ║ flight, plus a status-ledger writer mirroring the proven packet-122       ║
     9	// ║ orchestration-status.log.                                                  ║
    10	// ║                                                                           ║
    11	// ║ Design: pure pool primitive (worker is INJECTED) + ledger helpers, fully  ║
    12	// ║ unit-tested. The real spawn worker and the CLI entry that wires it        ║
    13	// ║ (arg parse, JSON-out, exit codes) live in fanout-run.cjs.                ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    19	// 1. IMPORTS
    20	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21	
    22	const fs = require('node:fs');
    23	const path = require('node:path');
    24	
    25	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	// 2. HELPERS
    27	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	
    29	function isRecord(value) {
    30	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    31	}
    32	
    33	function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
    34	  if (value instanceof Date) {
    35	    return value.toISOString();
    36	  }
    37	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
    38	    return value;
    39	  }
    40	  return new Date().toISOString();
    41	}
    42	
    43	function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
    44	  const n = Number(value);
    45	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
    46	    return 1;
    47	  }
    48	  return Math.floor(n);
    49	}
    50	
    51	function labelFor(item, index) {
    52	  if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
    53	    return item.label;
    54	  }
    55	  return `item-${index}`;
    56	}
    57	
    58	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	// 3. CORE LOGIC
    60	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Run a single pool item through the worker and capture its result or error.
    64	 *
    65	 * Never throws: a worker rejection is captured as a `rejected` result so one
    66	 * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
    67	 *
    68	 * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
    69	 * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
    70	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
    71	 * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
    72	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
    73	 * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
    74	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
    75	 */
    76	async function settleItem({ item, index, worker, now, onEvent }) {
    77	  const label = labelFor(item, index);
    78	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    79	  const startedAtMs = Date.now();
    80	
    81	  if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    82	    onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, at: startedAtIso });
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  try {
    86	    const output = await worker(item, { index });
    87	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    88	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
    89	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    90	      onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
    91	    }
    92	    return {
    93	      label,
    94	      status: 'fulfilled',
    95	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    96	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
    97	      duration_ms: durationMs,
    98	      output,
    99	    };
   100	  } catch (error) {
   101	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   102	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   103	    const normalizedError = {
   104	      name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
   105	      message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
   106	    };
   107	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   108	      onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
   109	    }
   110	    return {
   111	      label,
   112	      status: 'rejected',
   113	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   114	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   115	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   116	      error: normalizedError,
   117	    };
   118	  }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
   123	 *
   124	 * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
   125	 * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
   126	 * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
   127	 *
   128	 * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
   129	 * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
   130	 * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).
   131	 * @param {Function} options.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
   132	 * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date/ISO timestamp.
   133	 * @param {Function} [options.onEvent] - Optional ledger-event callback.
   134	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} { results: [...ordered], summary: { total,
   135	 *   succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   136	 * @throws {TypeError} If items is not an array or worker is not a function.
   137	 */
   138	function runCappedPool(options) {
   139	  if (!isRecord(options)) {
   140	    throw new TypeError('runCappedPool options must be an object');
   141	  }
   142	  const { items, worker } = options;
   143	  if (!Array.isArray(items)) {
   144	    throw new TypeError('items must be an array');
   145	  }
   146	  if (typeof worker !== 'function') {
   147	    throw new TypeError('worker must be a function');
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const concurrency = normalizeConcurrency(options.concurrency);
   151	  const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
   152	  const onEvent = typeof options.onEvent === 'function' ? options.onEvent : undefined;
   153	  const results = new Array(items.length);
   154	
   155	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   156	    if (items.length === 0) {
   157	      resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   158	      return;
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    let nextIndex = 0;
   162	    let active = 0;
   163	    let resolved = false;
   164	
   165	    const pump = () => {
   166	      if (resolved) {
   167	        return;
   168	      }
   169	      if (nextIndex >= items.length && active === 0) {
   170	        resolved = true;
   171	        resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   172	        return;
   173	      }
   174	      while (active < concurrency && nextIndex < items.length) {
   175	        const index = nextIndex;
   176	        nextIndex += 1;
   177	        active += 1;
   178	        settleItem({ item: items[index], index, worker, now, onEvent })
   179	          .then((result) => {
   180	            results[index] = result;
   181	          })
   182	          .catch((error) => {
   183	            // settleItem never throws, but stay defensive so the pump never wedges.
   184	            results[index] = {
   185	              label: labelFor(items[index], index),
   186	              status: 'rejected',
   187	              error: { name: 'PoolError', message: String(error) },
   188	            };
   189	          })
   190	          .finally(() => {
   191	            active -= 1;
   192	            pump();
   193	          });
   194	      }
   195	    };
   196	
   197	    pump();
   198	  });
   199	}
   200	
   201	/**
   202	 * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
   203	 *
   204	 * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
   205	 * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   206	 */
   207	function buildPoolSummary(results) {
   208	  const total = results.length;
   209	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   210	  const failed = total - succeeded;
   211	  return {
   212	    results,
   213	    summary: {
   214	      total,
   215	      succeeded,
   216	      failed,
   217	      all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
   218	    },
   219	  };
   220	}
   221	
   222	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	// 4. STATUS LEDGER
   224	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   228	 *
   229	 * Generalizes the packet-122 `orchestration-status.log`: one line per lineage
   230	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
   231	 *
   232	 * @param {string} ledgerPath - Path to the JSONL ledger file.
   233	 * @param {Object} entry - A serializable event object.
   234	 */
   235	function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry) {
   236	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(ledgerPath), { recursive: true });
   237	  fs.appendFileSync(ledgerPath, `${JSON.stringify(entry)}\n`);
   238	}
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Write the orchestration summary JSON for a completed fan-out run.
   242	 *
   243	 * @param {string} summaryPath - Path to the summary JSON file.
   244	 * @param {Object} summary - Summary payload (e.g. the pool summary plus
   245	 *   salvage counts and per-lineage rollups).
   246	 */
   247	function writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, summary) {
   248	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(summaryPath), { recursive: true });
   249	  fs.writeFileSync(summaryPath, `${JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)}\n`);
   250	}
   251	
   252	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   253	// 5. EXPORTS
   254	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   255	
   256	module.exports = {
   257	  runCappedPool,
   258	  settleItem,
   259	  buildPoolSummary,
   260	  appendStatusLedger,
   261	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
   262	};

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs | sed -n '1,360p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Cross-Lineage Merge                          ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Input:  CLI args (--loop-type, --artifact-dir).                          ║
     5	// ║ Output: JSON to stdout.                                                  ║
     6	// ║ Exit:   0=ok, 1=script error, 3=input validation error.                 ║
     7	// ║                                                                          ║
     8	// ║ Reads every {artifact-dir}/lineages/{label}/ sub-packet and produces:   ║
     9	// ║   research: deduplicated deep-research-findings-registry.json +          ║
    10	// ║             fanout-attribution.md                                        ║
    11	// ║   review:   severity-rollup deep-review-findings-registry.json           ║
    12	// ║             (strongest-restriction: any lineage P0 → merged FAIL) +     ║
    13	// ║             fanout-attribution.md                                        ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	const fs = require('node:fs');
    19	const path = require('node:path');
    20	
    21	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22	// 1. HELPERS
    23	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24	
    25	function inputError(message) {
    26	  const err = new Error(message);
    27	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    28	  return err;
    29	}
    30	
    31	function jsonOut(payload) {
    32	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
    33	}
    34	
    35	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    36	  const args = {};
    37	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    38	    const token = argv[i];
    39	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    40	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    41	    }
    42	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    43	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    44	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    45	      args[key] = true;
    46	    } else {
    47	      args[key] = next;
    48	      i += 1;
    49	    }
    50	  }
    51	  return args;
    52	}
    53	
    54	function ensureString(args, key) {
    55	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    56	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
    57	  }
    58	  return args[key];
    59	}
    60	
    61	function tryReadJson(filePath) {
    62	  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return null;
    63	  try {
    64	    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
    65	  } catch {
    66	    return null;
    67	  }
    68	}
    69	
    70	function readStateLog(stateLogPath) {
    71	  if (!fs.existsSync(stateLogPath)) return [];
    72	  const lines = fs.readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean);
    73	  return lines.flatMap((line) => {
    74	    try {
    75	      return [JSON.parse(line)];
    76	    } catch {
    77	      return [];
    78	    }
    79	  });
    80	}
    81	
    82	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    83	// 2. RESEARCH MERGE
    84	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85	
    86	/**
    87	 * Merge research findings registries from all lineages.
    88	 * Deduplicates by findingId; cross-model attribution via lineage labels.
    89	 * Returns the merged registry object.
    90	 */
    91	function mergeResearchRegistries(lineageData) {
    92	  const findingById = new Map();
    93	
    94	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
    95	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.keyFindings)) continue;
    96	    for (const finding of registry.keyFindings) {
    97	      const id = finding.id || finding.title;
    98	      if (!id) continue;
    99	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   100	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   101	        // Add this lineage as a contributing source
   102	        if (!existing._lineages) existing._lineages = [];
   103	        if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   104	      } else {
   105	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   106	      }
   107	    }
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   111	  const openQuestionsById = new Map();
   112	  const ruledOutById = new Map();
   113	
   114	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   115	    if (!registry) continue;
   116	    for (const q of registry.openQuestions ?? []) {
   117	      const id = q.id || q.question;
   118	      if (!id) continue;
   119	      if (!openQuestionsById.has(id)) openQuestionsById.set(id, { ...q, _lineages: [label] });
   120	      else openQuestionsById.get(id)._lineages.push(label);
   121	    }
   122	    for (const d of registry.ruledOutDirections ?? []) {
   123	      const id = d.id || d.direction;
   124	      if (!id) continue;
   125	      if (!ruledOutById.has(id)) ruledOutById.set(id, { ...d, _lineages: [label] });
   126	    }
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  const totalIters = lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => {
   130	    return sum + (registry?.metrics?.iterationsCompleted ?? 0);
   131	  }, 0);
   132	
   133	  const avgConvergence =
   134	    lineageData.length > 0
   135	      ? lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => sum + (registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? 0), 0) /
   136	        lineageData.length
   137	      : 0;
   138	
   139	  return {
   140	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   141	    openQuestions: [...openQuestionsById.values()],
   142	    resolvedQuestions: [],
   143	    keyFindings: mergedFindings,
   144	    ruledOutDirections: [...ruledOutById.values()],
   145	    metrics: {
   146	      iterationsCompleted: totalIters,
   147	      openQuestions: openQuestionsById.size,
   148	      resolvedQuestions: 0,
   149	      keyFindings: mergedFindings.length,
   150	      convergenceScore: Math.round(avgConvergence * 1000) / 1000,
   151	      coverageBySources: {},
   152	    },
   153	  };
   154	}
   155	
   156	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   157	// 3. REVIEW MERGE  (strongest-restriction)
   158	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   159	
   160	const SEVERITY_RANK = { P0: 3, P1: 2, P2: 1 };
   161	
   162	/**
   163	 * Merge review findings registries with strongest-restriction severity rollup.
   164	 * Any lineage with an active P0 finding causes the merged result to be FAIL.
   165	 * Deduplication is by findingId; cross-lineage P0 wins if any lineage reports it.
   166	 */
   167	function mergeReviewRegistries(lineageData) {
   168	  const findingById = new Map();
   169	
   170	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   171	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.openFindings)) continue;
   172	    for (const finding of registry.openFindings) {
   173	      if (finding.status !== 'active') continue;
   174	      const id = finding.findingId || finding.title;
   175	      if (!id) continue;
   176	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   177	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   178	        // Strongest-restriction: escalate to highest severity seen across lineages
   179	        if ((SEVERITY_RANK[finding.severity] ?? 0) > (SEVERITY_RANK[existing.severity] ?? 0)) {
   180	          findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [...(existing._lineages || []), label] });
   181	        } else {
   182	          if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   183	        }
   184	      } else {
   185	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   186	      }
   187	    }
   188	  }
   189	
   190	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   191	  const activeP0 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P0' && f.status === 'active').length;
   192	  const activeP1 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P1' && f.status === 'active').length;
   193	  const activeP2 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P2' && f.status === 'active').length;
   194	
   195	  // Strongest-restriction verdict
   196	  let mergedVerdict;
   197	  if (activeP0 > 0) {
   198	    mergedVerdict = 'FAIL';
   199	  } else if (activeP1 > 0) {
   200	    mergedVerdict = 'CONDITIONAL';
   201	  } else {
   202	    mergedVerdict = 'PASS';
   203	  }
   204	
   205	  return {
   206	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   207	    mergedVerdict,
   208	    openFindings: mergedFindings,
   209	    resolvedFindings: [],
   210	    findingsBySeverity: { P0: activeP0, P1: activeP1, P2: activeP2 },
   211	    openFindingsCount: mergedFindings.length,
   212	    resolvedFindingsCount: 0,
   213	    activeP0,
   214	    activeP1,
   215	    activeP2,
   216	  };
   217	}
   218	
   219	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   220	// 4. ATTRIBUTION
   221	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Build fanout-attribution.md summarizing per-lineage convergence, iters, salvage, model.
   225	 */
   226	function buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType) {
   227	  const lines = [
   228	    `# Fan-Out Attribution`,
   229	    ``,
   230	    `Loop type: **${loopType}**  |  Lineage count: **${lineageData.length}**`,
   231	    ``,
   232	    `| Label | Kind | Model | Iterations | Convergence | Salvaged | Verdict |`,
   233	    `|-------|------|-------|-----------|-------------|----------|---------|`,
   234	  ];
   235	
   236	  for (const { label, registry, stateRecords, kind, model } of lineageData) {
   237	    const iters = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'iteration').length;
   238	    const salvage = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'salvaged_from_stdout').length;
   239	    const convergenceScore = registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? registry?.convergenceScore ?? 'n/a';
   240	    const verdict =
   241	      loopType === 'review'
   242	        ? registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P0 > 0
   243	          ? 'FAIL'
   244	          : registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P1 > 0
   245	          ? 'CONDITIONAL'
   246	          : 'PASS'
   247	        : 'n/a';
   248	    lines.push(`| ${label} | ${kind ?? 'unknown'} | ${model ?? 'default'} | ${iters} | ${convergenceScore} | ${salvage} | ${verdict} |`);
   249	  }
   250	
   251	  lines.push('');
   252	  return lines.join('\n');
   253	}
   254	
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	// 5. MAIN
   257	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   258	
   259	function main() {
   260	  const args = parseArgs();
   261	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   262	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   263	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   264	  }
   265	  const artifactDir = ensureString(args, 'artifactDir');
   266	  const lineagesDir = path.join(artifactDir, 'lineages');
   267	
   268	  if (!fs.existsSync(lineagesDir)) {
   269	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineages directory — nothing to merge', merged: 0 });
   270	    return;
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  const labelDirs = fs.readdirSync(lineagesDir).filter((entry) =>
   274	    fs.statSync(path.join(lineagesDir, entry)).isDirectory(),
   275	  );
   276	
   277	  if (labelDirs.length === 0) {
   278	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineage subdirs found', merged: 0 });
   279	    return;
   280	  }
   281	
   282	  // Load per-lineage data
   283	  const registryName =
   284	    loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-findings-registry.json' : 'deep-research-findings-registry.json';
   285	  const stateLogName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-state.jsonl' : 'deep-research-state.jsonl';
   286	  const summaryPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   287	  const orchestrationSummary = tryReadJson(summaryPath) ?? {};
   288	
   289	  const lineageData = labelDirs.map((label) => {
   290	    const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, label);
   291	    const registry = tryReadJson(path.join(lineageDir, registryName));
   292	    const stateRecords = readStateLog(path.join(lineageDir, stateLogName));
   293	    // Infer kind/model from state log executor records
   294	    const executorRecord = stateRecords.find((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'executor_start');
   295	    return {
   296	      label,
   297	      lineageDir,
   298	      registry,
   299	      stateRecords,
   300	      kind: executorRecord?.kind ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.kind ?? 'unknown',
   301	      model: executorRecord?.model ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.model ?? 'unknown',
   302	    };
   303	  });
   304	
   305	  const lineagesWithRegistry = lineageData.filter((d) => d.registry !== null);
   306	
   307	  let mergedRegistry;
   308	  if (loopType === 'review') {
   309	    mergedRegistry = mergeReviewRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry);
   310	  } else {
   311	    mergedRegistry = mergeResearchRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry);
   312	  }
   313	
   314	  // Write merged registry to base artifact dir (replacing single-executor path)
   315	  const mergedRegistryPath = path.join(artifactDir, registryName);
   316	  fs.writeFileSync(mergedRegistryPath, JSON.stringify(mergedRegistry, null, 2), 'utf8');
   317	
   318	  // Write attribution markdown
   319	  const attributionPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'fanout-attribution.md');
   320	  fs.writeFileSync(attributionPath, buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType), 'utf8');
   321	
   322	  jsonOut({
   323	    status: 'ok',
   324	    loop_type: loopType,
   325	    merged_lineages: lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   326	    skipped_no_registry: lineageData.length - lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   327	    merged_registry_path: mergedRegistryPath,
   328	    attribution_path: attributionPath,
   329	    ...(loopType === 'review'
   330	      ? { merged_verdict: mergedRegistry.mergedVerdict, active_p0: mergedRegistry.activeP0, active_p1: mergedRegistry.activeP1 }
   331	      : { key_findings: mergedRegistry.keyFindings?.length ?? 0 }),
   332	  });
   333	}
   334	
   335	// Exports for unit testing
   336	module.exports = { mergeResearchRegistries, mergeReviewRegistries, buildAttributionMd };
   337	
   338	if (require.main === module) {
   339	  try {
   340	    main();
   341	  } catch (err) {
   342	    const code = err && err.code === 'INPUT_VALIDATION' ? 3 : 1;
   343	    jsonOut({
   344	      status: 'error',
   345	      error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
   346	      code: err && err.code ? err.code : 'SCRIPT_ERROR',
   347	    });
   348	    if (code === 1) {
   349	      process.stderr.write(
   350	        JSON.stringify({ error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err), stack: err && err.stack }) + '\n',
   351	      );
   352	    }
   353	    process.exit(code);
   354	  }
   355	}

codex
A likely tension is emerging: the current pool is a pure K-at-a-time queue with no dependency graph, while 007 correctly names `dependsOn/order` as a real minimal extension. I’m re-reading the remaining parser and runner lines before deciding whether that’s already honestly scoped or a finding.

   323	export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
   324	  const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
   325	  if (!parsed.success) {
   326	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
   327	  }
   328	
   329	  const config = parsed.data;
   330	
   331	  // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
   332	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   333	    const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, ...executorSubset } = entry;
   334	    try {
   335	      parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
   336	    } catch (err) {
   337	      if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
   338	        throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   339	          issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
   340	        });
   341	      }
   342	      throw err;
   343	    }
   344	  });
   345	
   346	  // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
   347	  const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
   348	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   349	    if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
   350	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   351	        issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
   352	      });
   353	    }
   354	    seenLabels.add(entry.label);
   355	  });
   356	
   357	  // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
   358	  const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
   359	  for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
   360	    if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
   361	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   362	        issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
   363	      });
   364	    }
   365	    expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
   366	  }
   367	
   368	  return config;
   369	}
   370	
   371	/**
   372	 * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
   373	 *
   374	 * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
   375	 * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
   376	 * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
   377	 *
   378	 * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
   379	 * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
   380	 */
   381	export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
   382	  const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
   383	  for (const entry of config.executors) {
   384	    if (entry.count === 1) {
   385	      lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
   386	      continue;
   387	    }
   388	    for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
   389	      lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });
   390	    }
   391	  }
   392	  return lineages;
   393	}

   114	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   115	
   116	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
   117	
   118	### Consolidated Setup Prompt for `:confirm` and No-Suffix Mode
   119	
   120	Use this block only when `execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"` or when no suffix was supplied and Q2 must ask for the execution mode.
   121	
   122	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   123	
   124	```
   125	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   126	
   127	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   128	   |-- ":auto"    -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   129	   |-- ":confirm" -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   130	   +-- No suffix  -> execution_mode = "ASK" (include Q2)
   131	
   132	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for target (review_target):
   133	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   134	   |     -> review_target = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   135	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   136	
   137	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   138	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   139	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   140	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   141	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   142	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   143	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   144	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   145	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   146	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   147	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   148	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   149	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch
   150	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   151	   |
   152	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   153	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   154	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   155	   |   - Review fan-out verdict: strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL)
   156	   |
   157	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=7, convergenceThreshold=0.10, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   158	
   159	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   160	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults
   161	   - The generated `deep-review-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
   162	
   163	   Parsing to config mapping:
   164	   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
   165	   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
   166	   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
   167	   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
   168	   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`

   171	   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time

   415	  --executor=native --count=1 --label=native \
   416	  --executor=cli-codex --model=o4-mini --count=2 \
   417	  --concurrency=3
   418	```
   419	
   420	> **Review fan-out verdict:** strongest-restriction applies — if any lineage has an active P0 finding, the merged verdict is FAIL regardless of other lineages' outcomes.

   112	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   113	
   114	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   115	   |-- ":auto"         -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   116	   |-- ":confirm"      -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   117	   +-- No suffix       -> execution_mode = "ASK"
   118	
   119	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for topic:
   120	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   121	   |     -> research_topic = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   122	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   123	
   124	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   125	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   126	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   127	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   128	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   129	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   130	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   131	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   132	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   133	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   134	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   135	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   136	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
   137	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   138	   |
   139	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   140	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   141	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   142	   |
   143	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   144	
   145	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   357	# Single-executor (default — unchanged)
   358	/deep:start-research-loop:auto "WebSocket reconnection strategies across browsers"
   359	/deep:start-research-loop:confirm "distributed caching patterns for microservices"
   360	/deep:start-research-loop:auto "API rate limiting approaches" --max-iterations 5 --convergence 0.10
   361	
   362	# Fan-out: two CLI lineages in parallel (cli-codex + cli-claude-code)
   363	/deep:start-research-loop:auto "caching patterns" \
   364	  --executor=cli-codex --model=o4-mini --label=codex \
   365	  --executor=cli-claude-code --model=claude-opus-4-8 --label=claude \
   366	  --concurrency=2
   367	
   368	# Fan-out: native (sequential agent dispatches) + CLI lineages mixed
   369	/deep:start-research-loop:auto "distributed systems consensus" \
   370	  --executor=native --count=1 --label=native \
   371	  --executor=cli-codex --model=o4-mini --count=2 \
   372	  --concurrency=3
   373	
   374	# Fan-out via JSON escape hatch
   375	/deep:start-research-loop:auto "WebRTC internals" \
   376	  --executors='[{"kind":"cli-codex","model":"o4-mini","label":"codex","count":3},{"kind":"cli-claude-code","model":"claude-opus-4-8","label":"claude","count":2}]' \
   377	  --concurrency=4
   378	```
   379	
   380	> **Note on native fan-out:** `--executor=native --count=N` dispatches N sequential `@deep-research` sub-agents with isolated artifact dirs. Not pooled. Best for count 1–3. For higher parallelism, use CLI executor kinds.

   113	}

   116	  if (EXECUTOR_COMMON_ENV_ALLOWLIST.has(key) || key.startsWith('LC_')) {
   117	    return true;
   118	  }

   124	}

   126	function splitDispatchStack(stack: string | undefined): string[] {
   127	  return (stack ?? '')
   128	    .split(':')
   129	    .map((entry) => entry.trim())
   130	    .filter((entry) => entry.length > 0);
   131	}
   132	
   133	function commandLineContainsBinary(commandLine: string, binary: string): boolean {
   134	  return commandLine
   135	    .split(/\0|\s+/)
   136	    .map((token) => token.trim().replace(/^['"]|['"]$/g, ''))
   137	    .filter((token) => token.length > 0)
   138	    .some((token) => basename(token) === binary);
   139	}

   141	function readLinuxAncestorCmdlines(startPid: number = process.ppid): string[] {
   142	  const cmdlines: string[] = [];
   143	  let currentPid = startPid;
   144	  const seen = new Set<number>();
   145	
   146	  while (currentPid > 1 && !seen.has(currentPid)) {
   147	    seen.add(currentPid);
   148	    const procDir = join('/proc', String(currentPid));
   149	    const cmdlinePath = join(procDir, 'cmdline');
   150	    const statPath = join(procDir, 'stat');
   151	
   152	    if (!existsSync(cmdlinePath) || !existsSync(statPath)) {
   153	      break;
   154	    }
   155	
   156	    try {
   157	      const cmdline = readFileSync(cmdlinePath, 'utf8').replace(/\0/g, ' ').trim();
   158	      if (cmdline.length > 0) {
   159	        cmdlines.push(cmdline);
   160	      }
   161	
   162	      const stat = readFileSync(statPath, 'utf8');
   163	      const closeParenIndex = stat.lastIndexOf(')');
   164	      const afterCommand = closeParenIndex === -1 ? '' : stat.slice(closeParenIndex + 2).trim();
   165	      const parentPid = Number(afterCommand.split(/\s+/)[1]);
   166	      if (!Number.isInteger(parentPid) || parentPid <= 0) {
   167	        break;
   168	      }
   169	      currentPid = parentPid;
   170	    } catch {
   171	      break;
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  return cmdlines;
   176	}

   179	  const paths: string[] = [];

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1073:        note: "Skill-owned contract fix (028): without this stage step, operators who do `git add research-report.md` lose the iteration trail (iterations/, deltas/, state.jsonl, strategy, etc.). Staging the whole artifact_dir keeps the audit trail intact. Operators retain commit authority and can `git restore --staged {path}` to unstage anything before commit. Failure is non-fatal — log warning and continue."

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:7:Model an iteration schedule as an ordered sequence of single-executor lineages (bands) inside the existing fan-out machinery, not as new intra-loop executor rotation. Reframe "nine times M2.7 then one time Opus" as two homogeneous bands the system already supports: band A executor `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` iterations 9; band B executor `claude-opus-4-8` iterations 1, `dependsOn` A. Add a thin `iterationPlan` flag (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) over the existing `executors` schema that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint, reusing `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts`. Per-band focus/dimensions become optional fields threaded into existing per-iteration prompt construction; merge precedence (Opus overrides MiniMax on conflict) is an ordered band-priority list consumed by `fanout-merge.cjs`, keeping severity rollup as an invariant above priority. Single-executor and homogeneous fan-out stay byte-identical; the mode is opt-in, absent-by-default. Reuse the deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it, front-loading research to lock the desugaring contract against the real fan-out APIs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:12:3. T3: design the `iterationPlan` schema and the iteration-plan/model-schedule flag — ordered bands, string sugar, validation, one-to-one desugaring into the `executors` lineage array.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:13:4. T4: specify desugaring and dispatch integration — `step_fanout_spawn`/`step_fanout_spawn_cli` consume desugared lineages through `fanout-pool.cjs`/`fanout-run.cjs` unchanged; per-lineage artifact-dir isolation, session id, per-kind state-dir per band.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:14:5. T5: specify ordered/sequential bands — minimal `dependsOn`/`order` extension so band B runs after band A with optional seed-context handoff, inside the existing pool driver (no ad-hoc shell, `/tmp`, or direct CLI loop).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:17:8. T8: specify merge precedence — ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` for conflict resolution, preserving the severity-rollup invariant for review and dedup/attribution for research.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:3:description: "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator requests a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) via /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and shared deep-loop-runtime, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool like packet 122 review did. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over existing fan-out via a thin iterationPlan flag desugaring one-to-one into ordered lineages plus minimal sequencing. Produces schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:11:    packet_pointer: "skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:14:    recent_action: "Authored Level-2 lean docs for iterationPlan model-schedule design phase"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:27:Design (spec only) a first-class, contract-compliant way for an operator to request a fixed-count heterogeneous per-iteration model schedule through the canonical `/deep:start-review-loop` and `/deep:start-research-loop` commands and the shared `deep-loop-runtime`. Motivating example: nine iterations on `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` then one on `claude-opus-4-8`, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool the way packet 122 review did. This phase produces the schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, and command-surface design; implementation is this phase's own future work (no code here).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:31:**Problem statement — the contract gap:** there is no supported way to express "run band A on model X for N iterations, then band B on model Y for M iterations" through the canonical commands. Existing fan-out (phases 001-006) runs homogeneous lineages concurrently and merges once all complete; it has no ordered heterogeneous per-iteration schedule and no ordered/seeded band sequencing, forcing operators back toward the packet 122 hand-rolled path.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:35:- Design a fixed-count heterogeneous per-iteration model-schedule capability expressible via the canonical `/deep:start-review-loop` and `/deep:start-research-loop` commands and the shared `deep-loop-runtime`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:36:- Schema/flag design: an `iterationPlan` of ordered bands (from-to or count; executor kind and model; optional focus/dimensions; optional dependsOn/order) plus the `iteration-plan` (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) flag desugaring one-to-one into the existing `executors` lineage array, integrated through `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts` (reuse-first; no parallel dispatcher).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:37:- Ordered/sequential bands: a minimal `dependsOn`/`order` extension so a later band runs only after an earlier completes, with optional seed-context handoff, enforced inside the existing pool driver (no ad-hoc shell).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:39:- Per-iteration/per-band executor audit and tracing (band and model attribution) in the JSONL state log, deltas, and findings registry; plus merge-precedence as an ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` preserving review severity rollup and research dedup/attribution.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:41:- A Level-2 lean phase spec plus ordered tasks and risks numbered `007`, reusing the packet deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:47:- True intra-lineage per-iteration executor rotation inside one continuous loop with cross-lineage iteration interleaving or band-synchronization barriers (rejected in favor of ordered homogeneous lineages unless desugaring proves insufficient).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:48:- Convergence-algorithm changes (newInfoRatio, Bayesian scoring) beyond making per-band max-iterations and ordered bands interoperate with existing convergence.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:49:- New executor kinds/providers/models beyond those registered (minimax, codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin, native) and changes to the single-executor default path or existing homogeneous fan-out merge semantics.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:56:- Merge precedence keeps the severity-rollup invariant (any active P0 from any band forces merged FAIL).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:61:- **Sequencing gap:** existing fan-out runs lineages concurrently and jumps to synthesis once all complete; band B after band A with optional seeding needs an ordered/`dependsOn` extension to `fanout-pool.cjs` that does not yet exist, so it may be underestimated as pure sugar. Mitigation: front-load research to confirm whether ordering is sugar over concurrency caps or a genuine minimal pool-driver delta, and scope it explicitly.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:62:- **Reuse-vs-rewrite drift:** pressure to match packet 122 per-iteration-dimension UX could tempt true intra-loop executor rotation (the hard path needing band-sync barriers and iteration interleaving). Mitigation: hold the ordered-homogeneous-lineage framing and keep intra-loop rotation out of scope unless desugaring is proven insufficient.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:63:- **Merge-precedence regression:** generalizing the any-P0-to-FAIL rule into ordered band-priority risks weakening strongest-restriction, since a lower-priority band P0 must still escalate. Mitigation: specify precedence as conflict-resolution only, with severity rollup preserved as an invariant above band priority.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:64:- **Contract-compliance illusion and two-consumer divergence:** a loosely specified band-priority or ordered layer could resemble the forbidden ad-hoc dispatcher, and research dedup/attribution versus review severity-rollup may fit one design unevenly. Mitigation: explicit per-forbidden-pattern mapping; require all dispatch inside `step_fanout_spawn` and the fan-out scripts; specify the band-priority and audit model for both consumers with differences called out.

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:447:          - claim_adjudication_active_count: "Count of active P0/P1 findings at the time of the last claim_adjudication event; 0 when none exist"

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:495:               recovery_strategy = "Reviewer must add typed claim-adjudication packets (claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, downgradeTrigger) for every active P0/P1 finding before STOP is allowed."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:498:             a) if iteration_count >= max_iterations: set decision = "STOP", stopReason = "maxIterationsReached", hard_stop_reason = "max_iterations_reached", preserve any failed legal-stop gates as terminal evidence, and proceed to synthesis without legal-stop veto
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:499:             b) if all dimensions clean AND required traceability protocols are covered AND p0_count == 0 AND p1_count == 0 AND coverage_age >= 1 AND all quality gates pass (evidence: every P0/P1 has file:line; scope: all findings within target; coverage: all dimensions reviewed): mark hard_stop_reason = "all_dimensions_clean" and promote to STOP candidate
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:502:             if stuck_count >= 2: STUCK_RECOVERY
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:509:               a) Rolling average (w=0.30, window=2, needs 2+ iterations):
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:512:               b) MAD noise floor (w=0.25, needs 3+ iterations):
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:524:               c) p0ResolutionGate: pass when activeP0 == 0; record activeP0
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:525:               d) evidenceDensityGate: pass when every active P0/P1 has concrete file:line evidence and no active P0/P1 relies only on inference; record avgEvidencePerFinding

codex
## Focus
DIMENSION: 007 design-phase soundness.
## Findings
P1, .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:36, The `iterationPlan` schema allows bands expressed by `count`, but the real fan-out API already uses `count` for replica expansion, while per-lineage iteration limits live in `iterations` (`executor-config.ts:291-299`, `:388-390`). That makes the 9+1 schedule ambiguous: a naive desugar could create nine replicas instead of one lineage capped at nine iterations. Fix: define band cardinality as `iterations` or explicitly map band `count`/range length to `lineage.iterations`, never `lineage.count`.

P1, .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:14, The ordered-band design says sequencing is enforced inside the existing pool driver, but native lineages bypass `fanout-run.cjs` entirely (`fanout-run.cjs:289-290`) and are dispatched by separate YAML `for_each` branches. Mixed native/CLI schedules therefore cannot be ordered by a pool-driver-only `dependsOn/order` extension. Fix: put sequencing in a coordinator above both CLI and native branches, or route native lineages through the same scheduler.

P1, .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:49, The spec treats `minimax` and bare `codex`/`claude`/`opencode`/`gemini`/`devin` as registered executor kinds, but the real executor enum is `native`, `cli-codex`, `cli-gemini`, `cli-claude-code`, `cli-opencode`, and `cli-devin` (`executor-config.ts:7`). The motivating MiniMax schedule is not desugarable without either a supported executor mapping or a new executor kind, which this line scopes out. Fix: use canonical executor kinds and provider-qualified models, or explicitly bring a `minimax` executor addition into scope.
## Verdict
The design is close, and it preserves the severity-rollup intent, but the desugaring contract needs these API-level gaps closed before implementation.
Review verdict: CONDITIONAL

===FINDINGS_JSON===

A naive 9+1 desugar could create nine replicas instead of one lineage capped at nine iterations.","fix":"Define band cardinality as iterations or explicitly map band count/range length to lineage.iterations, never lineage.count."},{"id":"f-p123-i14-02","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md","line":14,"issue":"The ordered-band design says sequencing is enforced inside the existing pool driver, but native lineages bypass fanout-run.cjs and are dispatched by separate YAML branches. Mixed native/CLI schedules cannot be ordered by a pool-driver-only dependsOn/order extension.","fix":"Put sequencing in a coordinator above both CLI and native branches, or route native lineages through the same scheduler."},{"id":"f-p123-i14-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md","line":49,"issue":"The spec treats minimax and bare codex/claude/opencode/gemini/devin as registered executor kinds, but the real executor enum uses native and cli-* kinds only. The motivating MiniMax schedule is not desugarable without either a supported executor mapping or a new executor kind, which this line scopes out.","fix":"Use canonical executor kinds and provider-qualified models, or explicitly bring a minimax executor addition into scope."}]

## Focus
DIMENSION: 007 design-phase soundness.
## Findings
P1, .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:36, The `iterationPlan` schema allows bands expressed by `count`, but the real fan-out API already uses `count` for replica expansion, while per-lineage iteration limits live in `iterations` (`executor-config.ts:291-299`, `:388-390`). That makes the 9+1 schedule ambiguous: a naive desugar could create nine replicas instead of one lineage capped at nine iterations. Fix: define band cardinality as `iterations` or explicitly map band `count`/range length to `lineage.iterations`, never `lineage.count`.

P1, .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:14, The ordered-band design says sequencing is enforced inside the existing pool driver, but native lineages bypass `fanout-run.cjs` entirely (`fanout-run.cjs:289-290`) and are dispatched by separate YAML `for_each` branches. Mixed native/CLI schedules therefore cannot be ordered by a pool-driver-only `dependsOn/order` extension. Fix: put sequencing in a coordinator above both CLI and native branches, or route native lineages through the same scheduler.

P1, .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:49, The spec treats `minimax` and bare `codex`/`claude`/`opencode`/`gemini`/`devin` as registered executor kinds, but the real executor enum is `native`, `cli-codex`, `cli-gemini`, `cli-claude-code`, `cli-opencode`, and `cli-devin` (`executor-config.ts:7`). The motivating MiniMax schedule is not desugarable without either a supported executor mapping or a new executor kind, which this line scopes out. Fix: use canonical executor kinds and provider-qualified models, or explicitly bring a `minimax` executor addition into scope.
## Verdict
The design is close, and it preserves the severity-rollup intent, but the desugaring contract needs these API-level gaps closed before implementation.
Review verdict: CONDITIONAL
